commands.review_summary:
The PR defaults Slack `member-info` action `userId` to the trusted inbound `requesterSenderId` when omitted and adds unit coverage for fallback, missing-sender, and explicit-user precedence.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +54. Total +61 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source. Current main exposes `userId` as optional in the shared message tool schema, but Slack dispatch requires it and deterministically throws when omitted.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling should wait for contributor-provided live Slack proof; no automated code repair is indicated from this review.

target.title: fix(slack): default member-info userId to inbound sender
target.body:
> **Draft:** code + unit/contract coverage are complete; holding for human-run real-behavior proof from a live Slack OpenClaw setup before marking ready (per CONTRIBUTING's external-PR proof gate).

## Summary

The Slack `message` tool's `member-info` action required a `userId` param **unconditionally** (`readStringParam(actionParams, "userId", { required: true })`), throwing `"userId required"` when the model invoked it with only `{action: "member-info", channel: "slack"}` — e.g. during a self / "who am I / who can talk to me" introspection turn.

- **Why it matters:** the `userId` schema field is already `Type.Optional`, so dispatch was stricter than the schema — the model legitimately omits `userId` and the call hard-fails. Discord/Feishu/WhatsApp already default this kind of self-introspection to the trusted inbound sender (`ctx.requesterSenderId`); Slack was the odd one out.
- **Outcome:** `member-info` now defaults `userId` to `ctx.requesterSenderId` (the server-injected, trusted inbound sender) when omitted, mirroring the existing channel-extension pattern. If neither an explicit `userId` nor an inbound sender is available, it throws a model-actionable error instead of the bare `"userId required"`. An explicit `userId` still wins over the sender default.
- **Out of scope:** no change to valid `member-info` calls that pass `userId`; Discord's identically-strict `member-info` left untouched (could follow for parity if maintainers want it).

## What reviewers should focus on

Whether defaulting to `ctx.requesterSenderId` is the desired semantic for Slack `member-info` (it's documented as trusted/server-injected and never model-controlled), and the error-message wording.

## Linked context

No upstream issue. Discovered while running OpenClaw companions downstream; clear repro + root cause described above. Happy to file an issue first if maintainers prefer.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** `member-info` failing with `userId required` on `{action, channel}`-only calls during introspection turns.
- **Real environment tested:** _Pending._ Marking this PR draft until I can attach proof from a live Slack-connected OpenClaw instance.
- **Automated evidence (supplemental):**
  - New unit tests in `extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts`: (1) defaults to inbound sender, (2) actionable error when no userId + no sender, (3) explicit userId wins.
  - `pnpm test:extension slack` → 99 files / 1242 tests pass.
  - `pnpm test:contracts:channels` → all channel-contract shards pass.
  - oxlint + tsc (slack extension) clean.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end against a live Slack workspace (the proof this section asks for) — to be added before un-drafting.

## Tests and validation

`pnpm test:extension slack`, `pnpm test:contracts:channels`, `npx oxlint extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.ts`, `tsc --noEmit` on the slack extension. New regression coverage added in the dispatch test file.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? **Yes** — `member-info` without `userId` now succeeds (self-introspection) instead of erroring; previously-erroring calls change outcome.
- Config / env / migration change? **No.**
- Security / auth / secrets / network / tool-exec change? **No** — `userId` defaults only to the trusted server-injected `ctx.requesterSenderId`, never a model-supplied value, so no cross-user enumeration path is opened.
- Highest-risk area / mitigation: the defaulting source; mitigated by using only `requesterSenderId` (documented trusted inbound id) and preserving explicit-param precedence.

## Current review state

Draft — next action is attaching live-setup real-behavior proof, then marking ready for review.
